findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Finsupp/Weight.lean:213` defines `def degree : (σ →₀ R) →+ R`, `:220` has `degree_eq_sum`, `:225` has `degree_single`, `:230` has `degree_eq_zero_iff`, `:238` has `le_degree`, and `:259` has `range_single_one`; PR line 33 defines `order α := α.sum fun _ n => n`.
findings.fix: Import `Mathlib.Data.Finsupp.Weight` and use `Finsupp.degree`; replace `order_single`, `order_add`, `order_eq_sum`, `apply_le_order`, `order_eq_zero_iff`, and first-order characterization with `Finsupp.degree_single`, `map_add`, `Finsupp.degree_eq_sum`, `Finsupp.le_degree`, `Finsupp.degree_eq_zero_iff`, and `Finsupp.range_single_one` as appropriate.
findings.issue: `MultiIndex.order` is definitionally the same total-coordinate sum as Mathlib’s `Finsupp.degree`, and most following lemmas restate existing API under parallel names.

findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Finsupp/Weight.lean:249` proves `theorem finite_of_degree_le [Finite σ] (n : ℕ) : {f : σ →₀ ℕ | degree f ≤ n}.Finite`; PR line 149 proves the same set finiteness for `order`.
findings.fix: State downstream facts using `Finsupp.finite_of_degree_le` after rewriting `order` to `Finsupp.degree`, rather than adding a new `DegreeLE` injection proof.
findings.issue: `finite_setOf_order_le` duplicates Mathlib’s bounded-degree finiteness theorem for finitely supported natural-valued functions.

summary: The PR duplicates Mathlib’s existing `Finsupp.degree` API for `ι →₀ ℕ`; the bounded-order finiteness theorem is also already present. Replace the parallel `MultiIndex.order` layer with the existing `Finsupp.degree` declarations or make only local aliases/notational shims where PDE-facing names are essential.
